questions.question: Which security goal ensures information is not disclosed to unauthorized users?
questions.description: Confidentiality keeps sensitive information visible only to authorized users or systems.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of multi-factor authentication?
questions.description: MFA combines proof types such as something you know, have, or are.

questions.question: Which attack attempts to trick users into revealing credentials?
questions.description: Phishing relies on deception through email, websites, calls, or messages.

questions.question: What does a firewall primarily control?
questions.description: Firewalls permit or deny traffic using rules based on addresses, ports, protocols, or application signals.

questions.question: Which practice reduces impact when one account is compromised?
questions.description: Least privilege gives users and services only the access needed to perform their tasks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of hashing a password before storage?
questions.description: A password hash lets the system verify a password without keeping the original password in the database.
